content.problemStatement:
In Chefland, a tax of rupees 101010 is deducted if the total income is **strictly greater** than rupees 100100100.

Given that total income is XXX rupees, find out how much money you get.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer XXX — your total income.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the amount of money you get.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X, the total income for that test case.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer on its own line – the amount of money you get after applying the tax rule.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
For each income:
- 5 ≤ 100 → no tax, output 5.
- 105 > 100 → tax 10, output 105‑10 = 95.
- 101 > 100 → tax 10, output 101‑10 = 91.
- 100 = 100 → no tax, output 100.
100 equals the threshold → no tax. 101 exceeds the threshold → 101‑10 = 91.

solution.approach: For each test case read X. If X is strictly greater than 100, subtract 10 (the tax) and output the result; otherwise output X unchanged. This is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
